That provisioning+header logic lives in `cmd/sim` (resolveTokens) and the ingestion header bridge — it was never ported into the e2e harness's separate `SimulateNode` implementation. The bf-2eub9 chain (fusion/tracker runtime) is correct; the gap is purely in the e2e test harness simulator, which is a separate parallel implementation.\\n\\n**Separate work needed (outside this chain's scope):** port the token path into the e2e harness — either (a) make `SimulateNode` call `/api/provision` and set `X-Spaxel-Token` (mirroring cmd/sim's resolveTokens), or (b) add a hello `\\\"token\\\"` field mints/validates against the same installSecret, or (c) have the harness pass a test-only validator override that admits the sim MACs. That provisioning+header logic lives in `cmd/sim` (resolveTokens) and the ingestion header bridge — it was never ported into the e2e harness's separate `SimulateNode` implementation. The bf-2eub9 chain (fusion/tracker runtime) is correct; the gap is purely in the e2e test harness simulator, which is a separate parallel implementation.\n\n**Separate work needed (outside this chain's scope):** port the token path into the e2e harness — either (a) make `SimulateNode` call `/api/provision` and set `X-Spaxel-Token` (mirroring cmd/sim's resolveTokens), or (b) add a hello `\"token\"` field mints/validates against the same installSecret, or (c) have the harness pass a test-only validator override that admits the sim MACs. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_635b2db72db04e7a97858fef","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t---\n2\tname: br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339\n3\tdescription: \"br close fails with \\\"Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input\\\" — fix by converting worker_sessions timestamps to RFC3339\"\n4\tmetadata: \n5\t  node_type: memory\n6\t  type: reference\n7\t  originSessionId: 7f606246-9db4-4a7f-be95-650beddf4385\n8\t---\n9\t\n10\tIn the spaxel workspace, `br close <id>` fails with `Error: Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input`. Root cause: bead-forge writes `worker_sessions.claimed_at` as a naive SQLite timestamp `'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'` (space-separated, no timezone), but `br close` parses it strictly as RFC3339 and chokes. ALL `worker_sessions` rows had this bad format and ZERO beads had ever been closed here — so `br close` was globally broken. (It writes `closed_at` correctly as RFC3339; only the claim-write path is buggy.) bead `bf-49fp` had been auto-retried ~11,885 times because no agent could close it. Related to the bead-forge bug tracked in CLAUDE.md ([[bead-forge]] repair-fix bead bf-2yj7 family).\n11\t\n12\t**Why:** `br close` reads the most recent unclosed claim for the bead and parses `claimed_at`; one bad value aborts the whole close. Retrying the bead does not help — the failure is deterministic in the db.\n13\t\n14\t**How to apply:** When `br close` throws \"premature end of input\" / \"Invalid claimed_at format\":\n15\t1. `br sync --flush-only` — checkpoint bead content (issues/comments) to JSONL.\n16\t2. Back up the db: `cp .beads/beads.db .beads/beads.db.bak`.\n17\t3. Convert only the naive rows (leaving valid RFC3339 untouched):\n18\t   ```python\n19\t   import sqlite3, re\n20\t   con = sqlite3.connect('.beads/beads.db')\n21\t   naive = re.compile(r'^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2} \\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2}$')\n22\t   rows = con.execute('SELECT rowid, claimed_at FROM worker_sessions').fetchall()\n23\t   con.execute('BEGIN')\n24\t   for rid, ts in rows:\n25\t       if naive.match(ts or ''):\n26\t           con.execute('UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=? WHERE rowid=?',\n27\t                       (ts[:10]+'T'+ts[11:]+'+00:00', rid))\n28\t   con.commit()\n29\t   ```\n30\t   (UTC confirmed safe: most recent claim's wall-clock matches `issues.updated_at` which is `+00:00`.)\n31\t4. `br close <id>` now succeeds; `br sync --flush-only`; remove the `.bak`.\n32\tNOTE: only the `worker_sessions` table (dispatch telemetry) is touched — never the `issues` table (bead content) or comments.\n33\t\n34\t**Update (2026-07-06):** the *global* break is fixed — 12269 of ~12271 `worker_sessions` rows are now valid RFC3339 and beads do close. But the claim-write bug **persists for new dispatches**: a fresh claim still writes `claimed_at` as naive `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`, length 19), so expect ~1–2 bad rows per new dispatch — the targeted repair above is **recurring**, not one-time. Confirmed again on bf-18yn (two length-19 rows from today's dispatch blocked the close; converting them unblocked it). The `sqlite3` binary IS available via Nix at `/nix/store/*-sqlite-3.51.2-bin/bin/sqlite3` (not on PATH) — a one-liner repair works:\n35\t```bash\n36\tSQLITE=$(ls -d /nix/store/*-sqlite-*-bin/bin/sqlite3 | head -1)\n37\t$SQLITE .beads/beads.db \"UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S+00:00',claimed_at) WHERE claimed_at LIKE '____-__-__ __:__:__';\"\n38\t```\n39\t(`python3 -c \"import sqlite3...\"` still works as a fallback if no Nix sqlite3 is present.)\n40\t\n41\t**Confirmed again 2026-07-07** (bf-601bz: 4 length-19 rows blocked close; scoped the WHERE to `bead_id='<id>'` for a targeted fix). Both RFC3339 suffixes are accepted by `br close`: the `+00:00` form above AND trailing `Z` via `strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ', claimed_at)` both worked. Remember to `br sync --flush-only` after closing so the checkpoint persists.\n42\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"cd3e8a6f-b58b-4b76-ae67-ad5546fbe384","uuid":"54a3b904-a733-4106-bb9b-17c262a0d1f5","timestamp":"2026-07-08T00:58:32.170Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/.claude/projects/-home-coding-spaxel/memory/br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339.md","content":"---\nname: br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339\ndescription: \"br close fails with \\\"Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input\\\" — fix by converting worker_sessions timestamps to RFC3339\"\nmetadata: \n  node_type: memory\n  type: reference\n  originSessionId: 7f606246-9db4-4a7f-be95-650beddf4385\n---\n\nIn the spaxel workspace, `br close <id>` fails with `Error: Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input`. Root cause: bead-forge writes `worker_sessions.claimed_at` as a naive SQLite timestamp `'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'` (space-separated, no timezone), but `br close` parses it strictly as RFC3339 and chokes. ALL `worker_sessions` rows had this bad format and ZERO beads had ever been closed here — so `br close` was globally broken. (It writes `closed_at` correctly as RFC3339; only the claim-write path is buggy.) bead `bf-49fp` had been auto-retried ~11,885 times because no agent could close it. Related to the bead-forge bug tracked in CLAUDE.md ([[bead-forge]] repair-fix bead bf-2yj7 family).\n\n**Why:** `br close` reads the most recent unclosed claim for the bead and parses `claimed_at`; one bad value aborts the whole close. Retrying the bead does not help — the failure is deterministic in the db.\n\n**How to apply:** When `br close` throws \"premature end of input\" / \"Invalid claimed_at format\":\n1. `br sync --flush-only` — checkpoint bead content (issues/comments) to JSONL.\n2. Back up the db: `cp .beads/beads.db .beads/beads.db.bak`.\n3. Convert only the naive rows (leaving valid RFC3339 untouched):\n   ```python\n   import sqlite3, re\n   con = sqlite3.connect('.beads/beads.db')\n   naive = re.compile(r'^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2} \\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2}$')\n   rows = con.execute('SELECT rowid, claimed_at FROM worker_sessions').fetchall()\n   con.execute('BEGIN')\n   for rid, ts in rows:\n       if naive.match(ts or ''):\n           con.execute('UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=? WHERE rowid=?',\n                       (ts[:10]+'T'+ts[11:]+'+00:00', rid))\n   con.commit()\n   ```\n   (UTC confirmed safe: most recent claim's wall-clock matches `issues.updated_at` which is `+00:00`.)\n4. `br close <id>` now succeeds; `br sync --flush-only`; remove the `.bak`.\nNOTE: only the `worker_sessions` table (dispatch telemetry) is touched — never the `issues` table (bead content) or comments.\n\n**Update (2026-07-06):** the *global* break is fixed — 12269 of ~12271 `worker_sessions` rows are now valid RFC3339 and beads do close. But the claim-write bug **persists for new dispatches**: a fresh claim still writes `claimed_at` as naive `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`, length 19), so expect ~1–2 bad rows per new dispatch — the targeted repair above is **recurring**, not one-time. Confirmed again on bf-18yn (two length-19 rows from today's dispatch blocked the close; converting them unblocked it). The `sqlite3` binary IS available via Nix at `/nix/store/*-sqlite-3.51.2-bin/bin/sqlite3` (not on PATH) — a one-liner repair works:\n```bash\nSQLITE=$(ls -d /nix/store/*-sqlite-*-bin/bin/sqlite3 | head -1)\n$SQLITE .beads/beads.db \"UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S+00:00',claimed_at) WHERE claimed_at LIKE '____-__-__ __:__:__';\"\n```\n(`python3 -c \"import sqlite3...\"` still works as a fallback if no Nix sqlite3 is present.)\n\n**Confirmed again 2026-07-07** (bf-601bz: 4 length-19 rows blocked close; scoped the WHERE to `bead_id='<id>'` for a targeted fix). Both RFC3339 suffixes are accepted by `br close`: the `+00:00` form above AND trailing `Z` via `strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ', claimed_at)` both worked. Remember to `br sync --flush-only` after closing so the checkpoint persists.\n","numLines":42,"startLine":1,"totalLines":42}}}
